patch.value: Investigate search command latency from persisted telemetry
Persisted telemetry shows search has a high p95 duration compared with other frequently used commands, which can slow agent loops and interactive triage.
Search latency is profiled with telemetry-backed reproduction; slow paths are optimized or documented with progress/diagnostic guidance; regression coverage protects the chosen fix.

value.author: cursor-agent
value.text: Duplicate check used pm search for search latency p95 telemetry performance slow. Results found closed vector store and telemetry work but no active search latency issue. Evidence shows search had 83 finishes with average 6753.6 ms and p95 32822 ms in persisted telemetry.

value.author: audit-agent
value.text: Audit observation: search command with semantic/hybrid mode experienced embedding timeout (30000ms) during batch search operations. This aligns with the existing issue description. The search falls back gracefully to keyword-only results when embeddings timeout.

author: codex-agent

patch.value: Telemetry-backed search latency issue is mitigated via bounded implicit semantic execution and deterministic fallback diagnostics; linked regression tests cover fallback classification and command behavior stability.
Address telemetry-backed search latency by bounding implicit auto-default semantic timeout windows and surfacing deterministic fallback diagnostics when keyword mode is selected.

patch.value: Implicit auto-default hybrid semantic execution now uses bounded timeout windows and deterministic fallback warning codes, reducing worst-case latency impact from repeated 30s semantic waits.
Telemetry-observed high p95 waits from implicit semantic timeouts are mitigated through bounded semantic attempts and explicit keyword fallback diagnostics.
Search command now applies bounded implicit semantic timeout options and emits classified fallback warnings (timeout/connection/error); linked regression suite passes.

message: Backfill close-readiness metadata for search latency remediation

value.author: codex-agent
value.text: Implemented telemetry follow-up 2026-04-27: bounded implicit auto-default hybrid semantic timeout windows and added deterministic fallback warnings (search_implicit_semantic_fallback:*:using_keyword_mode). Verification: pm test pm-bhmu --run --progress passed linked regression suite (4 files, 63 tests).

value.author: codex-agent
value.text: Latency strategy keeps explicit semantic/hybrid mode behavior unchanged while tightening only implicit auto-default hybrid execution to protect interactive loops.

value.author: codex-agent
value.text: When semantic fallback is automatic, classify fallback reasons in machine-readable warnings so telemetry and operators can distinguish timeout versus connectivity pressure.

author: codex-agent
